Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not bounce the weights off your chest. Keep your feet planted on the floor.
Pose.Name: Incline Dumbbell Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Clavicular Head - Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff injury, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Set an adjustable bench to 30-45 degrees. Sit on the bench, hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward. Lie back with dumbbells at chest level.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward and slightly inward until your arms are extended (but not locked).
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your upper chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Keep your elbows at about a 45-degree angle to your body—not flared straight out. This protects your shoulders.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bouncing the dumbbells off the chest.
Letting dumbbells drift too far apart.

Pose.safety: Keep your body in a straight line. Place your hands slightly wider than shoulders on an elevated surface. Lower your chest toward the surface with control.
Pose.Name: Incline Push-Up
Pose.Equipment: Bench, Step, or Sturdy Elevated Surface
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps, Core

steps.step: Place your hands on an elevated surface (bench, step) slightly wider than shoulder-width. Walk your feet back until your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Inhale as you slowly lower your chest toward the surface, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the surface or your elbows reach 90 degrees.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
The higher the surface, the easier the push-up. Start with a high bench and gradually lower the surface as you get stronger.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not bounce the weights off your chest. Keep your feet planted.
Pose.Name: Flat Dumbbell Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Sternal Head - Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff injury, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench, feet planted on the floor. Hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until your arms are extended (but not locked).
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Keep your shoulders pinned back against the bench. Don't let them round forward.

Pose.safety: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Place your hands slightly wider than shoulders. Lower your chest toward the floor with control.
Pose.Name: Standard Push-Up
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps, Core

steps.step: Start in a high plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, body in a straight line from head to heels.
Inhale as you slowly lower your chest toward the floor, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the floor or your elbows reach 90 degrees.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
If full push-ups are too difficult, perform them on your knees while keeping your body straight from knees to head.

Pose.safety: Keep your body in a straight line. Place your hands on an elevated surface with your feet on the floor. Lower your chest toward the floor with control.
Pose.Name: Decline Push-Up
Pose.Equipment: Bench or Step for Feet Elevation
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps, Core

steps.step: Place your feet on a bench or step. Walk your hands forward until your body forms a straight line from head to heels. Hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Inhale as you slowly lower your chest toward the floor, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the floor or your elbows reach 90 degrees.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Decline push-ups are more challenging than standard push-ups. Start with a low elevation and increase height as you get stronger.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat on the floor. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not bounce the weights. Keep your elbows at a comfortable angle.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Floor Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie on your back on the floor with knees bent. Hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until your arms are extended (but not locked).
Pause for 1 second at the top.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells until your upper arms touch the floor. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
The floor limits your range of motion, which protects your shoulders. This is a great alternative if you have shoulder pain.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement. Do not lock your elbows. Lower the dumbbells with control. Avoid overstretching.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Fly (Light)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Sternal Head), Anterior Deltoids (Stabilizer)
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff tear, pec tear

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench, feet planted. Hold dumbbells above your chest with palms facing each other, elbows slightly bent.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells out to your sides in an arc, keeping the slight bend in your elbows.
Lower until you feel a stretch in your chest (upper arms parallel to floor or slightly below).
Exhale as you bring the dumbbells back together in an arc, squeezing your chest at the top. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Use lighter weight than you think you need. Dumbbell flies are an isolation exercise—heavy weight can injure your shoulders.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to swing the weights.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Control the cables—do not let them snap back. Squeeze your chest at the peak of the movement.
Pose.Name: Cable Crossover (Light)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Dual Handles, Light Weight
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest Focus), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Set both pulleys to the highest position. Attach D-handles. Stand in the center, holding one handle in each hand with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you pull the handles downward and together in front of your hips, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Stand with one foot slightly forward for better balance. Keep your core engaged and back straight.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the bar to your upper chest with control. Do not bounce the bar. Keep your feet planted.
Pose.Name: Incline Barbell Press
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Light Plates, Adjustable Bench, Squat Rack
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff injury, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Set an adjustable bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie back, grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack the bar with a spotter if possible.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your upper chest (just below the collarbone), keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Exhale as you press the bar back to the starting position, squeezing your upper chest.
Perform 3 sets of 10 reps. Do not lock your elbows at the top.
Keep your shoulders pinned back against the bench. Don't let them round forward during the press.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bouncing the bar off the chest.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not bounce the weights. Keep your elbows at a comfortable angle.
Pose.Name: Incline Dumbbell Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps

steps.step: Set an adjustable bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie back with dumbbells at your shoulders, palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward and slightly inward until arms are extended.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the top, squeezing your upper chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion than barbells. Bring the dumbbells together at the top for a better squeeze.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not overstretch. Use light weight for flies.
Pose.Name: Incline Dumbbell Fly
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest - Clavicular Head), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Set bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie back with dumbbells above your chest, palms facing each other, elbows slightly bent.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells out to your sides in an arc, keeping the slight bend in your elbows.
Lower until you feel a stretch in your upper chest (upper arms parallel to floor or slightly below).
Exhale as you bring the dumbbells back together in an arc. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
The incline position emphasizes the upper chest. Keep your shoulders pinned back throughout the movement.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the bar to your mid-chest with control. Do not bounce the bar. Keep your feet planted.
Pose.Name: Flat Barbell Bench Press
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Light Plates, Flat Bench, Squat Rack, Spotter
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff injury, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench with feet planted. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack with a spotter.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your mid-chest (nipple line), keeping elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Exhale as you press the bar back to the starting position explosively.
Perform 3 sets of 10 reps. Do not lock your elbows at the top.
Keep your shoulders pinned back and down throughout the press. Imagine you're trying to bend the bar—this engages your lats.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bouncing the bar off the chest.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not bounce the weights. Keep your feet planted.
Pose.Name: Flat Dumbbell Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench, feet planted. Hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until arms are extended.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the top, squeezing your chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Bring the dumbbells together at the top for an extra chest squeeze. Dumbbells allow for a more natural range of motion than barbells.

Pose.safety: Keep your body in a straight line. Wear a weighted vest securely. Lower your chest toward the floor with control. Do not sag your hips.
Pose.Name: Push-Up (Weighted Vest)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major, Anterior Deltoids, Triceps, Core

steps.step: Put on a weighted vest. Start in a high plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulders, body straight.
Inhale as you slowly lower your chest toward the floor, keeping elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the floor or elbows reach 90 degrees.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to start. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
If you can't complete 15 reps with good form, reduce the vest weight or go back to bodyweight push-ups.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the bar to your lower chest with control. Do not bounce the bar. Keep your feet secured.
Pose.Name: Decline Barbell Press
Pose.Equipment: Decline Bench, Barbell, Squat Rack, Spotter
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack with a spotter.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your lower chest (sternum area), keeping elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Exhale as you press the bar back to the starting position explosively.
Perform 3 sets of 10 reps. Do not lock your elbows at the top.
Decline presses target the lower chest effectively. Keep your shoulders pinned back against the bench.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bouncing the bar off the chest.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not bounce the weights. Keep your feet secured.
Pose.Name: Decline Dumbbell Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie on a decline bench with feet secured. Hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until arms are extended.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the top, squeezing your lower chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Bring the dumbbells together at the top for a better chest contraction. The decline angle reduces shoulder involvement.

Pose.safety: Keep your body in a straight line. Place your feet on an elevated surface. Lower your chest toward the floor with control.
Pose.Name: Decline Push-Up
Pose.Equipment: Bench or Step for Feet Elevation
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps, Core

steps.step: Place your feet on a bench or step. Walk your hands forward until your body forms a straight line. Hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Inhale as you slowly lower your chest toward the floor, keeping elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the floor or elbows reach 90 degrees.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to start. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
Decline push-ups increase the load on your upper body. Start with a low elevation (e.g., 6 inches) and increase height as you get stronger.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Control the cables—do not let them snap back. Squeeze your chest at the peak of the movement.
Pose.Name: Cable Crossover
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Set both pulleys to the highest position. Attach D-handles. Stand in the center, holding one handle in each hand with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you pull the handles downward and together in front of your hips, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest as hard as possible.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Keep your chest up and shoulders back. Imagine you're hugging a large barrel—this helps engage the inner chest.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not overstretch. Use light to moderate weight.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Fly
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Sternal Head), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench, feet planted. Hold dumbbells above your chest with palms facing each other, elbows slightly bent.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells out to your sides in an arc, keeping the slight bend in your elbows.
Lower until you feel a stretch in your chest (upper arms parallel to floor or slightly below).
Exhale as you bring the dumbbells back together in an arc. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Imagine you're hugging a tree—this mental cue helps maintain the proper arc and elbow bend.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the pad. Squeeze your chest at the peak of the movement. Control the weight—do not let it slam.
Pose.Name: Pec Deck Machine
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Sit on the pec deck machine with your back pressed against the pad. Place your forearms on the pads with elbows at 90 degrees.
Exhale as you bring the pads together in front of your chest, squeezing your chest muscles.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest as hard as possible.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
Keep your shoulders down and back throughout. Don't let them round forward as you squeeze.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Use a spotter for heavy sets. Lower the bar to your upper chest with control. Do not bounce.
Pose.Name: Incline Barbell Press (Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Adjustable Bench, Squat Rack, Spotter
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps

steps.step: Set bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie back, grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Unrack with a spotter. Keep shoulders pinned back.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your upper chest (just below collarbone) over 2-3 seconds.
Exhale as you press the bar explosively back to the starting position.
Perform 4 sets of 8 reps. Do not lock elbows at the top.
At advanced weight levels, form is critical. Use a spotter, keep your back flat, and don't bounce the bar.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control. Squeeze your upper chest at the top. Use a spotter for heavy weight.
Pose.Name: Incline Dumbbell Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps

steps.step: Set bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie back with dumbbells at your shoulders, palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward and slightly inward until arms are extended.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your upper chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level over 2-3 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps.
Bring the dumbbells together at the top for maximum upper chest contraction. Control the descent—don't let gravity drop them.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and back straight. Control the cables—do not let them snap back. Squeeze your upper chest at the peak.
Pose.Name: High-to-Low Cable Fly
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest - Clavicular Head)

steps.step: Set both pulleys to the highest position. Attach D-handles. Stand in the center, holding one handle in each hand with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you pull the handles downward and together at waist height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the peak, squeezing your upper chest.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
High-to-low flies target the upper chest specifically. Focus on the squeeze at the bottom—imagine you're crushing something between your palms.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not overstretch. Use moderate weight.
Pose.Name: Incline Dumbbell Fly
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Set bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie back with dumbbells above your chest, palms facing each other, elbows slightly bent.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells out to your sides in an arc, keeping the slight bend in your elbows.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your upper chest.
Exhale as you bring the dumbbells back together in an arc. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Don't go too deep—stop when you feel a stretch, not pain. The incline position emphasizes the upper chest.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Use a spotter for heavy sets. Lower the bar to your mid-chest with control. Do not bounce.
Pose.Name: Flat Barbell Bench Press (Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Flat Bench, Squat Rack, Spotter
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff tear, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench with feet planted. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Unrack with a spotter. Keep shoulders pinned back.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your mid-chest (nipple line) over 2-3 seconds, elbows at 45 degrees.
Exhale as you press the bar explosively back to the starting position.
Perform 4 sets of 8 reps. Do not lock elbows at the top.
At advanced weight, every rep must be controlled. Use leg drive, keep your upper back tight, and never bounce the bar.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control. Squeeze your chest at the top. Control the descent.
Pose.Name: Flat Dumbbell Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench, feet planted. Hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until arms are extended.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level over 2-3 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps.
Dumbbells require more stability than barbells. Keep your shoulders pinned back and don't let them round forward.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Control the cables—do not let them snap back. Squeeze your chest at the peak.
Pose.Name: Cable Fly (Mid)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Dual Handles, Pulleys at Mid Height
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Set both pulleys to mid height (shoulder level). Attach D-handles. Stand in the center, holding one handle in each hand with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you pull the handles together in front of your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Mid-height cable flies target the entire chest evenly. Focus on squeezing at the peak and controlling the return.

Pose.safety: Keep your body in a straight line. Lower your chest toward the floor with control over 3-4 seconds. Do not sag your hips.
Pose.Name: Push-Up (Slow Eccentric)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major, Anterior Deltoids, Triceps, Core

steps.step: Start in a high plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulders, body straight.
Inhale as you slowly lower your chest toward the floor over 4 seconds, keeping elbows at 45 degrees.
Pause for 1 second at the bottom.
Exhale as you push explosively back to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
The slow eccentric (lowering) phase builds muscle size. Count "one one thousand, two one thousand" on the way down.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Use a spotter for heavy sets. Lower the bar to your lower chest with control. Do not bounce.
Pose.Name: Decline Barbell Press (Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Decline Bench, Heavy Barbell, Squat Rack, Spotter
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie on a decline bench with feet secured. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Unrack with a spotter.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your lower chest (sternum area) over 2-3 seconds.
Exhale as you press the bar explosively back to the starting position.
Perform 4 sets of 8 reps. Do not lock elbows at the top.
Decline presses allow you to handle heavy weight while protecting the shoulders. Keep your shoulders pinned back.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control. Squeeze your lower chest at the top.
Pose.Name: Decline Dumbbell Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie on a decline bench with feet secured. Hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until arms are extended.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your lower chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Decline presses emphasize the lower chest. Keep your shoulders pinned back—don't let them round forward.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Control the cables—do not let them snap back. Squeeze your lower chest at the peak.
Pose.Name: Low-to-High Cable Fly
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Dual Handles, Low Pulleys
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Set both pulleys to the lowest position. Attach D-handles. Stand in the center, holding one handle in each hand with palms facing up.
Exhale as you pull the handles upward and together at chest height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Low-to-high flies target the lower chest and upper chest tie-in. Focus on squeezing at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your body upright and close to the bars. Lean forward slightly to emphasize the chest. Lower yourself with control to 90 degrees.
Pose.Name: Dips (Chest Focused)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff injury, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Grip parallel bars with palms facing inward. Jump or step up to support your weight with arms straight.
Inhale as you slowly lower your body, leaning forward (about 30 degrees) to emphasize the chest. Lower until elbows reach 90 degrees.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to the starting position.
Perform 3 sets of 10 reps. Keep your core engaged and shoulders down.
To target the chest, lean forward. To target triceps, keep your body upright. Never go past 90 degrees with your elbows.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Control the cables—do not let them snap back. Squeeze your chest hard at the peak.
Pose.Name: Cable Crossover (Heavy - Advanced)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Set both pulleys to the highest position. Attach D-handles. Stand in the center with one foot slightly forward.
Exhale as you pull the handles downward and together in front of your hips, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest as hard as possible.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 4 sets of 12 reps.
Use a weight that allows you to squeeze at the peak. Heavy weight is useless if you can't contract the muscle.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the pad. Squeeze your chest at the peak of the movement. Control the weight—do not let it slam.
Pose.Name: Pec Deck Machine
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Sit on the pec deck machine with your back pressed against the pad. Place your forearms on the pads with elbows at 90 degrees.
Exhale as you bring the pads together in front of your chest, squeezing your chest muscles.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest as hard as possible.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
Don't let the weight stack touch between reps. Keep constant tension on your chest for better growth.

Pose.safety: Keep your body in a straight line. Place your hands closer than shoulder-width. Lower your chest toward the floor with control.
Pose.Name: Close-Grip Push-Up
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids, Core

steps.step: Start in a high plank position with hands placed directly under your shoulders (narrower than shoulder-width).
Inhale as you slowly lower your chest toward the floor, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the floor or elbows reach 90 degrees.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to start. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your body. This variation emphasizes the triceps and inner chest.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Squeeze the dumbbells together as hard as possible. Control the movement—do not drop the weights.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Squeeze Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest), Triceps

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench. Hold two dumbbells together with palms facing each other, pressing them together tightly.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward, keeping them squeezed together throughout the movement.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your inner chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level, keeping them squeezed together. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
The squeeze press is an inner chest builder. Focus on squeezing the dumbbells together as hard as you can throughout the entire rep.

Pose.safety: Use a spotter for heavy sets. Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the bar to your upper chest with control. Do not bounce. This is a strength movement.
Pose.Name: Incline Barbell Press (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Adjustable Bench, Squat Rack, Spotter, Belt
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps

steps.step: Set bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie back, grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Unrack with a spotter. Keep shoulders pinned back.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your upper chest over 2-3 seconds.
Exhale as you press the bar explosively back to the starting position.
Perform 5 sets of 5 reps. Do not lock elbows at the top.
At expert weight levels, incline press is a primary strength movement. Use a spotter, keep your back flat, and never sacrifice form.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control over 3-4 seconds. Squeeze your upper chest at the top.
Pose.Name: Incline Dumbbell Press (Slow Eccentric - Expert)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps

steps.step: Set bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie back with dumbbells at your shoulders, palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until arms are extended (1 second).
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your upper chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level over 4 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
The 4-second eccentric builds upper chest size. Count "one one thousand, two one thousand" on the way down.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Control the cables—do not let them snap back. Squeeze your upper chest at the peak.
Pose.Name: High-to-Low Cable Fly (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest - Clavicular Head)

steps.step: Set both pulleys to the highest position. Attach D-handles. Stand in the center with one foot slightly forward.
Exhale as you pull the handles downward and together at waist height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for 3 seconds at the peak, squeezing your upper chest.
Inhale as you slowly return over 3 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 12 reps.
The 3-second squeeze at the peak is key for upper chest development. Hold the contraction as long as you can.

Pose.safety: This is a drop set. Do not rest between drops. Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Control the weight—do not let it drop.
Pose.Name: Incline Dumbbell Fly (Drop Set - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Adjustable Bench (30-45 degrees), Dumbbells, Multiple Weight Increments
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest)

steps.step: Set bench to 30-45 degrees. Set up three weight increments: heavy, medium, light.
Perform 12 reps with heavy weight. Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Immediately grab medium weight. Perform 8 more reps without rest.
Immediately grab light weight. Perform 6 more reps. Perform 3 drop sets total.
Drop sets on incline flies create an incredible upper chest pump. Perform them at the end of your chest workout.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back flat. Use a landmine attachment. Squeeze your upper chest at the peak. Control the movement throughout.
Pose.Name: Landmine Press
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Upper Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps, Core

steps.step: Load one end of a barbell into a landmine attachment. Stand facing the loaded end with feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the end of the barbell with both hands or one hand.
Exhale as you press the barbell upward and slightly inward, keeping your core braced.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your upper chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps per side.
Landmine presses are shoulder-friendly and target the upper chest. Keep your back flat and don't arch.

Pose.safety: Use a spotter for heavy sets. Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the bar to your mid-chest with control. Do not bounce. This is a strength movement.
Pose.Name: Flat Barbell Bench Press (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Flat Bench, Squat Rack, Spotter, Belt
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff tear, pectoral tear

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench with feet planted. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Unrack with a spotter. Keep shoulders pinned back.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your mid-chest (nipple line) over 2-3 seconds.
Exhale as you press the bar explosively back to the starting position.
Perform 5 sets of 3-5 reps. Do not lock elbows at the top.
At expert level, bench press is a competition lift. Use leg drive, keep your upper back tight, and never bounce the bar.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control over 3-4 seconds. Squeeze your chest at the top.
Pose.Name: Flat Dumbbell Press (Slow Eccentric - Expert)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest), Anterior Deltoids, Triceps

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench, feet planted. Hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until arms are extended (1 second).
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level over 4 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
The 4-second eccentric builds chest size. Don't let the dumbbells drop—control them all the way down.

Pose.safety: This is a drop set. Do not rest between drops. Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Squeeze your chest at the peak.
Pose.Name: Cable Fly (Mid, Drop Set - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Dual Handles, Multiple Weight Increments
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Middle Chest)

steps.step: Set both pulleys to mid height (shoulder level). Set up three weight increments: heavy, medium, light.
Perform 12 reps with heavy weight. Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Immediately reduce weight to medium. Perform 8 more reps without rest.
Immediately reduce to light weight. Perform 6 more reps. Perform 3 drop sets total.
Drop sets on cable flies create an incredible chest pump. Keep your chest up and shoulders back throughout.

Pose.safety: Keep your body upright and close to the bars. Lean forward slightly to emphasize the chest. Use a weight belt for added resistance. Lower with control.
Pose.Name: Weighted Dips (Chest Focused - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Dip Bars, Weight Belt, Weight Plates
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff injury, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Attach a weight plate to a dip belt. Grip parallel bars with palms facing inward. Jump or step up to support your weight.
Inhale as you slowly lower your body, leaning forward (about 30 degrees) to emphasize the chest. Lower until elbows reach 90 degrees over 2-3 seconds.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to the starting position explosively.
Perform 4 sets of 8 reps. Keep your core engaged and shoulders down.
Weighted dips are a powerful chest builder. Never go past 90 degrees with your elbows—this protects your shoulders.

Pose.safety: This is an explosive plyometric movement. Land softly with bent elbows. Warm up thoroughly before attempting. Have a soft surface.
Pose.Name: Push-Up (Clap/Plyometric - Expert)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Explosive Power), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids, Core
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Wrist injury, shoulder impingement, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Start in a high plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulders, body straight.
Inhale as you lower your chest toward the floor.
Exhale as you push explosively to launch your hands off the floor. Clap your hands together mid-air.
Land softly with bent elbows to absorb the impact. Immediately go into the next rep. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Clap push-ups are advanced. Master regular push-ups first. If you can't clap, start with explosive push-ups (lifting hands off the floor without clapping).

Pose.safety: Use a spotter for heavy sets. Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the bar to your lower chest with control. Do not bounce.
Pose.Name: Decline Barbell Press (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Decline Bench, Heavy Barbell, Squat Rack, Spotter, Belt
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie on a decline bench with feet secured. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Unrack with a spotter.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your lower chest (sternum area) over 2-3 seconds.
Exhale as you press the bar explosively back to the starting position.
Perform 5 sets of 5 reps. Do not lock elbows at the top.
Decline presses are often stronger than flat presses. Use a spotter and keep your shoulders pinned back.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Lower the dumbbells with control over 3-4 seconds. Squeeze your lower chest at the top.
Pose.Name: Decline Dumbbell Press (Slow Eccentric - Expert)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie on a decline bench with feet secured. Hold dumbbells at your shoulders with palms facing forward.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward until arms are extended (1 second).
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your lower chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level over 4 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
The 4-second eccentric builds lower chest size. Control the descent—don't let gravity drop the weights.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Control the cables—do not let them snap back. Squeeze your lower chest at the peak. Use heavy weight with control.
Pose.Name: Low-to-High Cable Fly (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Dual Handles, Low Pulleys
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Set both pulleys to the lowest position. Attach D-handles. Stand in the center with one foot slightly forward.
Exhale as you pull the handles upward and together at chest height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for 3 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest.
Inhale as you slowly return over 3 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 12 reps.
Low-to-high flies target the lower chest and the tie-in between chest and shoulders. The 3-second squeeze builds definition.

Pose.safety: Keep your body upright and close to the bars. Lean forward to emphasize the lower chest. Use a weight belt for added resistance. Lower with control.
Pose.Name: Chest Dip (Weighted - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Dip Bars, Weight Belt, Heavy Plates
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff injury, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Attach heavy weight to a dip belt. Grip parallel bars with palms facing inward. Lean forward (30-45 degrees) to emphasize the chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower your body until elbows reach 90 degrees over 2-3 seconds.
Exhale as you push through your palms to return to the starting position explosively.
Perform 4 sets of 10 reps. Keep your core engaged and shoulders down.
Chest dips are one of the best lower chest builders. Never go past 90 degrees with your elbows—this protects your shoulders.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Lower the dumbbells with control. Do not overstretch. Use moderate weight for flies.
Pose.Name: Decline Dumbbell Fly
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Lower Chest), Anterior Deltoids

steps.step: Lie on a decline bench with feet secured. Hold dumbbells above your chest with palms facing each other, elbows slightly bent.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells out to your sides in an arc, keeping the slight bend in your elbows.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your lower chest.
Exhale as you bring the dumbbells back together in an arc. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Decline flies target the lower chest. Don't go too deep—stop when you feel a stretch, not pain.

Pose.safety: This is a drop set. Do not rest between drops. Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout. Squeeze your chest hard at the peak.
Pose.Name: Cable Crossover (Heavy, Drop Set - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Dual Handles, Multiple Weight Increments
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest)

steps.step: Set both pulleys to the highest position. Set up three weight increments: heavy, medium, light.
Perform 12 reps with heavy weight. Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Immediately reduce weight to medium. Perform 8 more reps without rest.
Immediately reduce to light weight. Perform 6 more reps. Perform 4 drop sets total.
Drop sets on cable crossovers are a finishing exercise. Perform them at the end of your chest workout for maximum pump.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the pad. Squeeze your chest at the peak. Control the slow eccentric phase. Do not let the weight stack slam.
Pose.Name: Pec Deck Machine (Slow Eccentric - Expert)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest)

steps.step: Sit on the pec deck machine with your back pressed against the pad. Place your forearms on the pads with elbows at 90 degrees.
Exhale as you bring the pads together in front of your chest over 1 second, squeezing your chest muscles.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak, squeezing your chest as hard as possible.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position over 4 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 15 reps.
The 4-second eccentric builds inner chest definition. Don't let the weight pull you back—control it.

Pose.safety: Keep the dumbbells squeezed together throughout the entire movement. Do not let them separate. Squeeze your inner chest at the top.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Squeeze Press (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest), Triceps

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench. Hold two dumbbells together with palms facing each other, pressing them together as hard as possible.
Exhale as you press the dumbbells upward, keeping them squeezed together throughout the movement.
Pause for 3 seconds at the top, squeezing your inner chest and the dumbbells together.
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level, keeping them squeezed together. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps.
The squeeze press builds the inner chest line. Squeeze the dumbbells together as if you're trying to crush them—this maximizes inner chest activation.

Pose.safety: Keep a slight bend in your elbow throughout. Control the cable—do not let it snap back. Squeeze your inner chest at the peak.
Pose.Name: Single-Arm Cable Fly
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Single Handle, High Pulley
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest - Unilateral)

steps.step: Set one pulley to the highest position. Attach a D-handle. Stand facing away from the cable, holding the handle with one hand.
Exhale as you pull the handle downward and across your body in an arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause for 3 seconds at the peak, squeezing your inner chest.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps per side.
Single-arm cable flies help correct strength imbalances. Focus on squeezing the chest, not just moving the cable.

Pose.safety: Keep your back flat against the bench. Use a close grip (hands shoulder-width or closer). Lower the bar to your lower chest with control. Do not bounce.
Pose.Name: Close-Grip Bench Press (Inner Chest Focus - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell, Flat Bench, Squat Rack, Spotter
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Pectoralis Major (Inner Chest), Triceps, Anterior Deltoids
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, elbow tendinitis, wrist injury

steps.step: Lie flat on a bench with feet planted. Grip the bar with hands shoulder-width apart (closer than normal bench). Unrack with a spotter.
Inhale as you slowly lower the bar to your lower chest (sternum area), keeping your elbows close to your body.
Exhale as you press the bar back to the starting position.
Perform 3 sets of 10 reps. Keep your shoulders pinned back throughout.
Close-grip bench press targets the inner chest and triceps. Keep your elbows tucked—don't let them flare.
